english: a sort of small tent Cs.
v. lcòg-tse (lcog tse).
a mineral (?) Med.

1. Lex. a musical instrument, Schr.: a bell. -- 2. Mil.: còṅ-la (cong la) skyur-ba (skyur ba) to push one down a precipice in order to kill him (the only meaning the context here will admit); cf. tsoṅ-dóṅ (tsong dong). -- 3. v. ɣcòṅ (gcong).
a small bowl or dish Sch.; v. caṅ-cè (cang ce).
jagged, indented, serrated.
col. for lcùṅ-mo (lcung mo).
Pth.: ṅu-bód (ngu bod) còṅ-ba (cong ba) acc. to the context: to raise loud lamentations, wailings (at funerals); perh. etymologically connected with c'o-ṅés (cho nges). Cf. ɣcòṅ-skad (gcong skad).
= cùṅ-źo (cung zho).
perh. = còṅ-còṅ (cong cong), Mil. còṅ-róṅ (cong rong) tsér-ma (tser ma).
ornament for the head, worn by kings, tiara, diadem, crown; the crest of gallinaceous birds.
Ld.-Glr., Schl. p. 29, a (?).
a mouthful, a gulp, a little Sch.; cf. co-ré (co re).
Thgy. childish prattle or babbling.

1. to apprehend, to grasp (with the understanding), to impress, gen. with yíd-la (yid la), on the mind, e.g. the doctrine Dzl.; also bka (bka) nan-ɣcágs (nan gcags) c'én-po (chen po) ɣnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) to give a thoroughly solid, impressive instruction; ɣcágs-po (gcags po) byéd-pa (byed pa) = ɣcágs-pa (gcags pa); with additional force: *do (do) ćág-po (cag po) j'e'-pa* (jhe' pa) C. to impress (to one's mind) as firm as a rock. -- 2. relative to persons it is synon. to c'ágs-pa (chags pa) to love.

W. *ćáṅ-po* (cang po) clever; lively, sprightly; W. also attentive to, regardful of; ɣcáṅ-po (gcang po) drúṅ-po (drung po) clever and sagacious Mil., ɣcaṅ-drúṅ (gcang drung) ldán-pa (ldan pa) id. Pth.; hence also ɣcaṅ (gcang) sbst. sagacity, cleverness; k'a-ɣcáṅ (kha gcang) clever words, clever speech Cs.; cf. also k'a-sbyáṅ (kha sbyang); W.: *ćaṅ ćó-će* (cang co ce) to watch for; to keep guard, to watch; *ćaṅ-rìg ćó-će* (cang rig co ce) to be very attentive, to listen with fixed attention, *ćaṅ-rìg-ćàn* (cang rig can), C. *ćáṅ-rìg-ćèn* (cang rig cen) very attentive; W.: *ćaṅ skúl-će* (cang skul ce) 1. to exhort, admonish 2. to wake, to rouse from sleep; *ćós-si ćaṅ-skúl táṅ-će* (chos si cang skul tang ce) to give religious exhortations, to hold parenetic lectures.
v. ɣcód-pa (gcod pa).

frq. beast of prey, Lat. fera, but more in a systematic sense, so that the cat, and even the dog may be included; Glr. po. ɣcán-(ɣ)zán (gcan (g)zan) tá-ma (ta ma) 'the last of the beasts of prey', the cat.

Cs. humbleness, servility, flattery, Sch. also untruth, lie; ɣcám-bui (gcam bui) ṅag (ngag), or ts'ìg (tshig) a servile speech; ɣcám-bu (gcam bu) smrá-ba (smra ba) to speak submissively Stg. (not much used).
1. Sch.: cut out, put out, knocked out, e.g. mig (mig) an eye (cf. bcàr-ba (bcar ba)). -- 2. Mil.?
ɣcál-ba (gcal ba), ɣcál-du (gcal du) bkrám-pa (bkram pa) to spread, display, lay out e.g. precious stones, jewels, on a table, on the ground, Glr., also Lex.
1. vb. v. ɣcìd-pa (gcid pa). -- 2. sbst. = ɣcìn (gcin); bśaṅ (bshang) ɣcì (gci) both kinds of alvine discharges. Dzl.

num. one; ɣcìg (gcig) kyaṅ (kyang) even but one; one and the same, dus (dus) ɣcìg-tu (gcig tu) at the same time (whereas dus (dus) cìg-na (cig na) once, one day, which however is also written dus (dus) ɣcìg-na (gcig na)); ɣcìg (gcig) byéd-pa (byed pa) to unite (vb. n.), to join (in an act), to act in concert; sole, alone; dear, beloved, yab (yab) ɣcìg (gcig) dear father! Glr.: śin-tu (shin tu) ɣdúṅ-bai (gdung bai) ma (ma) ɣcìg (gcig) my own (only) beloved mother! somebody, some one Dzl., ɣcìg... (gcig ...) ɣcìg (gcig) the one -- the other, somebody or other, very frq.; ɣcìg-gis (gcig gis) ɣcìg (gcig), ɣcìg-la (gcig la) ɣcìg (gcig) etc. one another, each other frq.; mi-ɣcìg (mi gcig) C. differing, different.
